findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (with a concrete proving command, e.g. `… --help | grep '<code-review-id-example>'` or a pytest assertion on the subparser description) that verifies the code-review example appears in the updated help text.
evidence: Scope: 'Update the subparser help text to include a code-review example alongside the plan-review one.'
ABSENCE: no AC reads 'help text includes a code-review example' and no proving command asserts on --help output.
findings.finding: The Scope section promises a help-text update ('Update the subparser help text to include a code-review example alongside the plan-review one'), but no Acceptance Criterion covers it and no proving command verifies it. The -k cli selector over the named test file will not catch a missing or wrong help-text change.
scenarios: An executor ships the admission logic correctly but leaves the help text unchanged or writes an incorrect example; all -k cli tests pass and the ticket is closed without the help-text change ever being verified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] The `criteria eval --help` output includes a code-review criterion id example; verified by `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k help_text` or `python -m rebar criteria eval --help | grep <example-id>`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define (or reference precisely) the physical prompt-id formula for code-review project criteria (e.g., `code-review-project-<name>`) — either add a `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` constant to `ids.py` with a gate-aware `criterion_prompt_id` overload, or document the exact function/constant the code-review branch of `calibrate_criterion` will call.
evidence: `criterion_prompt_id` signature: `def criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str) -> str` — no `gate_key` param, always returns `f'plan-review-{criterion_id.replace(".","-")}'`
Child ticket rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 references `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review")` but this overload does not exist in `ids.py`
No `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` constant found anywhere in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/` or `src/rebar/llm/code_review/`

`_code_review_prompt_id` in `eval_solver.py:187-201` validates against the built-in `code-review-{OVERLAY_IDS}` set and has no path for `project.*` logical ids — it returns `None` for any non-`code-review-` prefixed id
findings.finding: The physical prompt-id mapping for a code-review project criterion is never defined in this plan or in the codebase. The plan dispatches admitted code-review ids to `calibrate_criterion`'s code-review branch (child rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8), which in turn calls `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key='code_review')` per the child ticket — but `criterion_prompt_id` in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` takes no `gate_key` parameter and unconditionally prepends `PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX='plan-review-'`. There is no `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` constant, no `code_review_criterion_prompt_id()` function, and no gate-aware overload anywhere in the codebase. The resolver this plan depends on is referenced but structurally undefined.
findings.location: Approach section ('dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path landed on the eval child [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8]'); `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36-42`; child ticket rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 description
scenarios: A project code-review criterion `project.foo` is admitted by the new preflight and dispatched to `calibrate_criterion`; the code-review branch tries to resolve its physical prompt-id but has no defined formula — `criterion_prompt_id('project.foo')` returns `'plan-review-project-foo'`, causing the eval spec to load from the wrong gate's path.
A developer implements the code-review branch in child 8153 by calling `criterion_prompt_id(cid, gate_key='code_review')` and gets a `TypeError` because the function signature is single-argument.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = 'code-review-'` to `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` and a gate-aware helper `def criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str, *, gate_key: str = 'plan_review') -> str` that selects the prefix based on `gate_key`, then reference it explicitly in the Approach section.

evidence: src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36-42: criterion_prompt_id takes one arg and unconditionally uses PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX
Ticket fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 status: open — the gate_key param is its deliverable
Ticket 8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 (the code-review calibrate branch) itself depends_on fc72, making fc72 a transitive prerequisite of this plan

findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path'; implicitly relies on criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review") from [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]
scenarios: If this CLI-layer ticket ships before fc72 and 8153 land, any dispatched code-review id would be mapped to plan-review-project-foo instead of code-review-project-foo, resolving the wrong eval spec

The live calibrate_criterion signature has no gate_key parameter. The plan neither specifies the expected post-delivery signature the CLI must pass gate_key into, nor states a hard ordering constraint that makes the capability available before this ticket executes.

If child fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 hasn't landed yet, criterion_prompt_id won't accept gate_key at all, causing a TypeError at call time.

Add a Scope note that this ticket depends on 8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 and fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 being merged first, and add both as ticket deps.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Note in the Approach (or Testing) that the solver-side fix in eval_solver._code_review_prompt_id (ticket 8153) must also land; currently the plan only describes the CLI-layer preflight change and relies on an unverified claim that the solver already handles project code-review ids.
evidence: eval_solver.py:187-201: _code_review_prompt_id checks only the static set {code-review-base, code-review-verify} | {code-review-<oid> for oid in OVERLAY_IDS} — no repo_root param, no effective_criteria call
Ticket 8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 Scope mentions extending eval_solver.py _code_review_prompt_id to consult effective_criteria(repo_root), but that ticket is open
Plan text does not note that this static-set limitation in the solver must also be fixed for the end-to-end path to work
findings.finding: The plan asserts the preflight will look ids up in 'code_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)', admit them, then dispatch via calibrate_criterion's code-review branch, which ultimately calls eval_solver._code_review_prompt_id. But _code_review_prompt_id (eval_solver.py:187-201) currently checks ONLY the static built-in set and does not accept or consult repo_root/effective_criteria — so a project code-review id (e.g. project.foo) would pass the new CLI preflight but be rejected as None in the solver arm.
findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path landed on the eval child'; eval_solver.py:187-201
scenarios: Author activates project.foo as a code-review criterion, runs `rebar criteria eval project.foo`, CLI preflight admits it (new behavior), calibrate_criterion dispatches to code-review arm with prompt_id='code-review-project-foo', eval_solver.run_case calls _code_review_prompt_id which returns None, falling through to the agentic case_store arm — wrong execution path

evidence: Ticket 8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 status=open (not merged); calibrate_criterion in eval.py:416-420 has no code-review branch today.
Ticket fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 status=open; ids.py:36 `criterion_prompt_id` has no `gate_key` parameter today.
Plan says 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path landed on the eval child' — asserts a capability that is not yet present.
Partially-verified: the target integration surface exists in design (tickets) but not yet in code.
findings.finding: The plan dispatches admitted code-review ids to `calibrate_criterion`'s code-review branch and to `criterion_prompt_id(..., gate_key="code_review")`, but both of those capabilities live in sibling stories (8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 and fc72-bf7e-7652-4228) that are currently **open** (not merged). The current `calibrate_criterion` has no code-review branch (eval.py:416-420 only checks the plan-review prefix), and `ids.py:36` has no `gate_key` parameter. Shipping this CLI change before those stories land would admit code-review ids into a dispatch path that silently misbehaves.
findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path landed on the eval child [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8]'; also references fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 for `criterion_prompt_id(gate_key="code_review")`

This ticket ships before fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 merges: `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key='code_review')` raises `TypeError` (unexpected keyword argument) at dispatch time.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ticket deps: `depends_on: [8153-e9b5-5706-49c8, fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]`. In the Approach, add a sentence: 'This story is gated on 8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 (code-review calibrate branch) and fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 (gate_key param) being merged first.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how failures in `code_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` (I/O errors, malformed config) are handled in the preflight — either document that a shared error wrapper already covers this, or add an explicit try/except that surfaces a clean CLI error message.
evidence: Plan calls `code_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` but specifies no try/except or graceful-degradation path for that call
Existing preflight (plan_review.registry.by_id) error behaviour is not described, so it is unknown whether a shared error-handling wrapper already covers this
findings.finding: The plan adds a new call to `code_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` in the CLI preflight but specifies no error handling if that call raises (e.g., malformed project config, missing file). A crash here would produce an unhandled traceback instead of the clean 'unknown' / 'ambiguous' error messages the plan promises.
findings.location: Approach section — 'Look the id up in BOTH the plan-review effective registry and the code-review effective registry'
scenarios: A project with a malformed code-review config triggers an unhandled exception in the preflight, producing a confusing traceback rather than a clean CLI error message
A repo_root with no code-review config causes effective_criteria to raise FileNotFoundError, crashing the CLI before the 'unknown id' path is reached
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the new `effective_criteria` call in the same error-handling pattern used for the existing plan-review registry lookup (or document explicitly that the outer CLI error handler already converts any registry exception to a user-facing message).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the plan-review preflight check (`registry.by_id(repo_root)`) with `plan_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` so both gate membership tests use the same lightweight API, consistent with the code-review lookup the plan already specifies.
evidence: Plan cites `code_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` for the code-review lookup, but the existing plan-review check uses `registry.by_id(repo_root)` (line 147 of the current file), which the plan does not say it will replace.
`by_id` is defined as `{c['id']: c for c in load_criteria(repo_root)}` (plan_review/registry.py:337-338), calling `load_criteria` which builds full descriptors from prompt files.
`effective_criteria` returns a lightweight `tuple[str, ...]` of active ids (plan_review/registry.py:257-264; code_review/registry.py:179-184) and is exactly what a membership-test preflight needs.
YAGNI violation: loading full prompt descriptors (I/O + parse) at CLI admission time does more than the current done-definition requires.
findings.finding: The Approach section implicitly continues using `registry.by_id(repo_root)` (plan-review) for the preflight id-existence check while using `code_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` for code-review. `by_id` calls `load_criteria`, which loads and parses full prompt descriptors for every active criterion — expensive work for a simple membership test. `effective_criteria` already exists on BOTH gates and is the lighter, consistent API for this purpose.
findings.location: Approach section; src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py:147 (existing code) vs plan's stated use of `effective_criteria` for code-review
scenarios: A project with many activated criteria pays prompt-file I/O costs on every `criteria eval` invocation before the actual calibration begins — work that is discarded immediately after the membership check.
A test that stubs the lighter `effective_criteria` but not `load_criteria` would silently pass through `by_id`'s real I/O, making the unit test tier harder to isolate.
findings.suggested_fix: In `_criteria_eval`, replace `args.criterion_id not in registry.by_id(repo_root)` with membership tests against `frozenset(plan_review_registry.effective_criteria(repo_root))` and `frozenset(code_review_registry.effective_criteria(repo_root))` — symmetric, lightweight, and sufficient for the preflight gate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify how gate identity established at the preflight is forwarded to `calibrate_criterion`: either (a) state that the child ticket's code-review branch infers the gate from the criterion id automatically so no new argument is needed, or (b) specify that a `gate=` parameter is threaded from the preflight to the call site.
evidence: Plan: 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path landed on the eval child (calibrate_criterion code-review branch [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8])'
calibrate_criterion signature at eval.py:384 has no gate/gate_key parameter
criterion_prompt_id in ids.py:36 is hard-wired to PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX with no gate parameter
The child ticket 8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 adds a code-review branch to calibrate_criterion, but whether it adds a gate param or infers from criterion id is not stated in this plan
findings.finding: The plan states 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path' but does not specify how the resolved gate identity (plan-review vs code-review) is communicated to `calibrate_criterion`, which currently accepts no gate/gate_key parameter. The child ticket (8153-e9b5-5706-49c8) adds a code-review branch that infers the gate from the criterion id internally — if that inference is the mechanism, the CLI just calls `calibrate_criterion(criterion_id, repo_root=repo_root)` unchanged, and the dispatch language is misleading. If instead a new `gate=` argument must be threaded, the plan needs to say so. The silence creates an implementation ambiguity.
findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path landed on the eval child (calibrate_criterion code-review branch [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8])'
scenarios: Implementer adds a `gate=` parameter to the calibrate_criterion call that doesn't exist yet (wiring error), or conversely does nothing and the child ticket's inference doesn't cover the new CLI path correctly.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence: 'The CLI passes `gate="code_review"` to `calibrate_criterion` (or, if the child's code-review branch already infers the gate from the criterion id, confirm the call site is unchanged).' This makes the seam between this ticket and the child explicit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale sentence explaining why dual-registry admission + one-gate resolution is the right approach (e.g., it mirrors the existing preflight pattern, adds no new CLI surface, and reuses the already-public effective_criteria APIs both gates expose).
evidence: Plan contains: 'Rejected alternative. Adding a `criteria eval --gate` flag — heavier UX for a rare case...' but no affirmative statement of why the chosen approach is positively good.
G6 criterion: 'The plan states a POSITIVE rationale for the chosen approach (its absence is a finding)'
findings.finding: The plan provides a rationale only for REJECTING the --gate flag alternative, but states no positive rationale for WHY the chosen dual-registry-admission + one-gate-resolution approach is a good fit — e.g., that it mirrors the existing preflight pattern, requires zero new CLI surface, and reuses already-public effective_criteria APIs. The absence of a positive rationale is a G6 criterion requirement.
scenarios: An implementer reading only the approach sees only what was rejected, not why the chosen design is correct — it cannot justify the approach to a reviewer.
findings.suggested_fix: Prepend to the Approach section: 'The preflight extension mirrors the existing single-registry guard — it uses the same effective_criteria API both gates already expose, adds zero new CLI surface, and keeps all gate-resolution logic server-side so the command stays simple.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a guard in the new CLI dispatch (or in `calibrate_criterion`) that detects when the resolved prompt-id comes from the wrong gate prefix and emits a located error, preventing silent misrouting if the code-review branch in child 8153 is absent or incomplete.
evidence: `calibrate_criterion` at `eval.py:416-420`: `prompt_id = criterion_id if criterion_id.startswith('plan-review-') else criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` — no code-review branch exists; any non-`plan-review-` id falls into `criterion_prompt_id()` which prefixes `plan-review-`
Plan states 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path LANDED ON the eval child' — implies a dependency but no fallback is defined for the unmet dependency case
AC only verifies exit-0 happy path and exit-1 rejection paths; no AC covers what happens if the code-review calibrate branch misbehaves or is absent
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for the case where a code-review id is admitted by the new preflight but the code-review branch of `calibrate_criterion` (landed in child rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8) is absent or raises an unexpected error. The current `calibrate_criterion` has only a plan-review branch (`eval.py:416-420`) — an admitted code-review id reaching it today will silently load the wrong eval spec via `criterion_prompt_id` (which produces a `plan-review-*` path). The plan specifies no guard, error message, or early-exit for this incomplete-dependency scenario.
findings.location: Approach section ('dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path landed on the eval child'); `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:416-420`
scenarios: Child rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 is merged after this plan's CLI change but has a bug in the code-review branch: the CLI admits `project.foo`, dispatches it, and `calibrate_criterion` silently routes it through the plan-review path, loading no-such-file and raising an opaque `EvalError` that doesn't tell the user the criterion was misrouted.
If shipped before child 8153 lands (partial deploy), a newly admitted code-review id produces an obscure 'absent fixture' error rather than 'code-review calibrate path not yet available'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
